reason: Enabling SELinux enforcing mode can cause services (including SSH) to fail if their contexts are not correctly labeled. While the default RHEL policy generally allows SSH, custom policies or mislabelled files could lock out remote access. The remediation also requires a reboot, increasing the impact. Therefore a manual review is needed to verify SELinux policy compliance before applying.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential loss of SSH or console access if SELinux policy blocks services; Service disruptions due to mislabelled files; Reboot required for changes to take effect
